author: Dr. Jennifer Adams
content:
TELEPHONE ENCOUNTER — 10/30/2021, 3:22 PM

Patient: Robert Morrison (MRN: MRN-78432156)
Caller: Patient's wife, Patricia Morrison

Reason for Call:
Mrs. Morrison reports mild increase in bilateral leg swelling over the past 4-5 days and requests guidance on management.

Clinical Discussion:
Mrs. Morrison states that her husband's ankles and lower legs have gradually become more swollen over the past several days. His weight this morning was 100.8 kg, up from his usual baseline of 98-99 kg. He has been compliant with his medications, including the recently uptitrated carvedilol (now taking 12.5mg AM and 25mg PM per Dr. Chen's instructions from September). He has been tolerating the higher carvedilol dose well — no dizziness, no excessive fatigue, no worsening dyspnea. Heart rate per home monitor has been running 68-74 bpm.

Regarding the edema: patient denies worsening dyspnea beyond his NYHA II baseline. No orthopnea — still sleeping comfortably with one pillow. No chest pain. No PND. No decreased urine output. He can still walk to the mailbox without stopping. His UTI from September resolved completely after the nitrofurantoin course. No recurrent urinary symptoms.

Mrs. Morrison notes they had houseguests visiting for her birthday last weekend and ate out at a restaurant twice, where sodium intake was likely higher than his usual restricted diet. She is concerned this may have triggered the fluid retention.

His most recent labs (drawn 9/24): Cr 1.88, eGFR 38, K 4.7, Na 137, A1c 7.8% (slight increase from 7.4%). Hemoglobin 11.0.

Assessment:
Mild volume overload, likely related to dietary sodium indiscretion. Weight up ~2 kg from baseline. No evidence of significant decompensation based on history — patient remains at NYHA II, no orthopnea, no PND. This is a recurrent pattern for this patient (similar episodes in July 2021 and January 2021).

Plan:
1. Increase furosemide from 40mg BID to 60mg BID for 5 days, then return to 40mg BID
2. Strict low-sodium diet (<2g/day) — reinforce with wife, provide home-cooking tips for entertaining
3. Fluid restriction 1.5L/day
4. Elevate legs when seated
5. Daily weights: target return to 98-99 kg within 5 days
6. Call back criteria: weight not improving after 3 days, weight increases >2 lbs in a day, new or worsening dyspnea, orthopnea, chest pain, or decreased urine output
7. If symptoms worsen acutely, proceed to ED
8. Continue all other medications including newly uptitrated carvedilol
9. Note: A1c increased to 7.8% from 7.4% — may need insulin adjustment. Will address at upcoming office visit in November
10. Schedule follow-up appointment in 2-3 weeks to reassess volume status and discuss A1c management

Mrs. Morrison verbalized understanding. She will call back if symptoms do not improve or worsen. She plans to resume strict home cooking and avoid restaurant meals for the next several weeks.

Communicated to Dr. Chen (PCP) and Dr. Wilson (Cardiology) via inbox.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Jennifer Adams
Date: 10/30/2021
